sanskrit:
प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च जना न विदुरासुराः।
न शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते॥
transliteration: pravṛttiṃ ca nivṛttiṃ ca janā na vidur āsurāḥ | na śaucaṃ nāpi cācāro na satyaṃ teṣu vidyate ||
literal_meaning: They do not know what to engage in and what to refrain from; neither cleanliness, nor good conduct, nor truth is found in them.
contextual_meaning: Krishna describes the psychological profile of the āsuric (demoniac) personality: a mind devoid of self-monitoring, ethical compass, or basic hygiene of thought and action—an inner chaos that externalizes as social harm.

shadow: fear of one's own capacity for disorder

psychological_state: Moral disintegration—an ego without superego or healthy introjects; impulse-ridden, shameless, and truth-avoidant.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling past an online thread celebrating cruelty or misinformation and feeling a wave of nausea mixed with recognition of one's own potential for ethical blindness.
anchor_text: Cultivate inner order: know right from wrong, keep mind, body, and action clean.
